criteria: The agent has read Section 2 completely to understand the score-based diffusion model framework, including the forward noising process, variance schedule, and the training objective for the score network.

The agent has read Section 2 to understand the Stable Diffusion architecture, including the encoder E, decoder D, and how the diffusion process operates in the compressed latent space z_t rather than the image space.

The agent has read Section 3 completely to understand Projected Langevin Dynamics and why it cannot be directly applied to stable diffusion models due to the constraint set C not being representable in the latent space.

The agent has read Section 4 completely to understand the core Latent Space Correction methodology: using the decoder's differentiable mapping to backpropagate constraint gradients from image space to latent space.

The agent has read Section 4.2 completely to understand Proximal Langevin Dynamics, the proximal operator definition, and how it generalizes the projection step to accommodate non-smooth regularizers.

The agent has read Section 4.3 completely to understand Algorithm 1, distinguishing the outer minimizer (Langevin step + proximal update) from the inner minimizer (gradient descent loop terminating when constraint violation falls below tolerance delta).

The agent has read Section 5 completely to understand the two proxy constraint correction methods: differentiable surrogates and differentiating through black-box simulators via DPO with stochastic perturbations.

The agent has read Section 6.1 completely to understand the microstructure generation experiment, including the dataset preparation (64x64 patches upscaled to 1024x1024), the porosity constraint definition, and the top-k projection operator.

The agent has read Section 6.2 completely to understand the metamaterial inverse design experiment, including the use of Abaqus as a non-differentiable external simulator, the DPO setup with M=10 perturbed samples, and the 5-iteration convergence target.

The agent has read Section 6.3 completely to understand the copyright-safe generation experiment, including the balanced dataset of 8,000 images, the ResNet50 classifier architecture, PCA on 512 features selecting 2 principal components, and the Lagrangian relaxation inner minimizer.
The agent has read Appendix D completely to understand the Augmented Lagrangian method (Algorithm 2) and its dual variable updates used for non-convex constraint sets in Sections 6.2 and 6.3.

The agent has read Figure 1 to check the microstructure generation results, specifically that the Latent (Ours) model achieves FID 13.5±3.1 and 0% P error > 10%, while Cond achieves FID 10.8±0.9 with 68.4% P error, and PDM achieves FID 30.7±6.8 with 0% P error.

The agent has read Figure 3 to check the metamaterial inverse design results: Latent (Ours) achieves MSE 1.4±0.6 and 5% invalid shapes, Bastek and Kochmann achieves MSE 6.4±4.6 and 20%, and Cond achieves MSE 7.1±4.5 and 55%.

The agent has read Figure 5 to check the copyright-safe generation results: Latent (Ours) achieves 90% constraint satisfaction and FID 65.1, while Cond achieves 67% and 61.2, and PDM achieves 71% and 75.3.
The plan includes the overall constrained stable diffusion framework with an outer loop over denoising timesteps and an inner loop for the proximal update to correct constraint violations.

The plan includes a strategy to load a pretrained Stable Diffusion model and integrate the constraint correction step into its reverse (denoising) process without modifying the score network or decoder weights.
The plan includes the gradient computation through the frozen decoder to backpropagate constraint violations from image space to the latent representation, as described in Equation 4.

The plan includes the proximal operator implementation as a gradient descent inner loop that jointly minimizes the constraint violation g(D(z_t)) and the distance term (1/2lambda)||D(z_t^i) - D(z_t^0)||^2.

The plan includes the microstructure generation experiment with the porosity constraint, including the dataset preprocessing (subsampling 64x64 patches upscaled to 1024x1024) and the top-k projection operator.
The plan includes the metamaterial inverse design experiment with DPO, specifying the use of M=10 perturbed samples for gradient estimation and Abaqus as the external FEM simulator.

The plan includes the copyright-safe generation experiment with the surrogate classifier, including fine-tuning a ResNet50 backbone classifier and implementing PCA on 512 features to define the projection target.

The plan includes the three baselines for each experiment: Conditional Diffusion Model (Cond), Projected Diffusion Models (PDM) for microstructure and copyright experiments, and Bastek and Kochmann for the metamaterial experiment.

The plan includes the Augmented Lagrangian method for non-convex constraint settings (metamaterial and copyright experiments), with planned updates for Lagrangian multipliers lambda and penalty parameter mu.
The plan specifies that the constraint correction is applied only selectively during the denoising process for the copyright experiment (initial stages only), rather than at every denoising step.

The code for the constrained optimization formulation of the latent diffusion reverse process has been implemented as described in Equation 3, reformulating each denoising step as a constrained problem minimize -log q(z_t | z_0) s.t. g(D(z_t)) = 0.

The code for the gradient computation through the decoder D has been implemented, computing the latent-space gradient as the chain rule product (partial D / partial z_t)^T times the image-space constraint gradient, enabling backpropagation from image space to latent space without modifying the decoder.

The code for the Proximal Langevin Dynamics update (Equation 5) has been implemented, with a pre-proximal step z_t' = z_t + gamma_t * grad log q(z_t | z_0) + sqrt(2*gamma_t)*epsilon followed by the proximal operator application to obtain the corrected latent z_hat_t.

The code for the proximal operator (Equation 6) has been implemented as prox_{lambda*g}(x_t) = argmin_y {g(y) + (1/2*lambda) * ||y - x_t||^2}, balancing constraint adherence with proximity to the current iterate.

The code for the modified proximal operator for the latent space (Equation 7) has been implemented, replacing the ambient-space proximal objective with prox_{lambda*g}(z_t) = argmin_y {g(D(y)) + (1/2*lambda)*||D(y) - D(z_t)||^2}, applying the decoder D within the objective.

The code for the outer minimizer of Algorithm 1 has been implemented as an iterative loop over denoising timesteps (t from T to 0), applying the Langevin sampling step followed by the proximal correction at each step.

The code for the inner minimizer of Algorithm 1 has been implemented as a gradient descent while-loop that continues until the constraint violation g(D(z_t^i)) falls below the tolerance delta, computing the gradient of the prox_objective (violation + distance) and updating z_t by subtracting g * lr.

The prox_objective function has been implemented to compute both the constraint violation term g(x_t^i) and the distance term (1/2*lambda)*||x_t^i - x_t^0||^2_2, returning their sum for use in the inner minimizer gradient computation.

The code for the DPO smoothed function estimation (Section 5) has been implemented, computing the Monte Carlo estimate phi_bar_epsilon(x_t) = (1/M) * sum_{m=1}^{M} phi(x_t + nu*epsilon^(m)) with M=10 perturbed samples for the metamaterial experiment.

The code for the DPO gradient estimator has been implemented as a finite-difference estimator nabla_{x_t} phi_bar_nu(x_t) = (1/nu) * E[phi(x_t + nu*epsilon) * epsilon], enabling gradient-based updates for black-box non-differentiable simulators.

The code for the DPO differentiable loss gradient has been implemented as nabla_{x_t} L(phi(x_t)) = -(phi_bar_epsilon(x_t) - target), which is used to update the latent variable z_t via proximal Langevin dynamics.

The code for the Augmented Lagrangian method (Algorithm 2, Appendix D) has been implemented with the inner loop computing the ALM objective L_ALM = ||y - D(z_t)|| + lambda*g(y) + (mu/2)*g(y)^2 and the dual variable updates lambda += mu*phi_tilde(y) and mu = min(alpha*mu, mu_max).
The code for loading and preprocessing the microstructure dataset has been implemented, including subsampling images to 64x64 patches and upscaling them to 1024x1024.

The code for computing the porosity of a generated microstructure image has been implemented as porosity = sum_{i,j} 1(x^{i,j} < 0), using an indicator function that counts pixels with negative values.

The code for the top-k projection operator for porosity has been implemented, solving P_C(x) = argmin_{y^{i,j}} sum_{i,j} ||y^{i,j} - x^{i,j}|| s.t. y^{i,j} in [-1,1] and sum_{i,j} 1(y^{i,j} < 0) = K, where K is the target number of porous pixels.

The code for integrating the Abaqus FEM simulator as an external black-box constraint evaluator has been implemented for the metamaterial inverse design experiment, calling the simulator to compute the stress-strain response of generated structures.

The code for loading and processing the metamaterial dataset from Bastek and Kochmann (periodic stochastic cellular structures with full-field data capturing buckling and contact interactions) has been implemented.

The code for computing the DPO loss for the metamaterial experiment has been implemented, using the Abaqus simulator to evaluate stress-strain responses for M perturbed samples and computing the gradient estimate for the proximal update.

The code for loading the balanced copyright dataset (8,000 images of Mickey Mouse and Jerry characters) and fine-tuning a stable diffusion model on these images (without discriminating between classes) has been implemented.

The code for the ResNet50-based copyright classifier has been implemented with the architecture: ResNet50 backbone followed by two fully connected layers reducing dimensionality from 2048 to 512 and then from 512 to a single scalar, with a Sigmoid activation for binary class probability output.

The code for fine-tuning the ResNet50 classifier to distinguish between Mickey Mouse (copyrighted) and Jerry (target) has been implemented, achieving classification accuracy greater than 87% on a held-out test set.

The code for PCA on the 512 features input to the last classifier layer has been implemented, selecting the two principal components that form well-defined clusters corresponding to Mickey Mouse and Jerry class labels.

The code for the copyright inner minimizer has been implemented to differentiate with respect to the projection of the noisy sample onto the centroid of the Jerry (target) cluster in the 2D PCA space, using Lagrangian relaxation and terminating based on proximity to the Jerry cluster.

The code for the selective projection schedule in the copyright experiment has been implemented, triggering the proximal update only during the initial stages of denoising and only when the classifier assigns a high probability to the sample being Mickey Mouse.
The code for the Conditional Diffusion Model (Cond) baseline has been implemented using text-conditioned constraint guidance with an identical Stable Diffusion architecture as the proposed method.

The code for the Projected Diffusion Model (PDM) baseline has been implemented following Christopher et al. [15], enforcing feasibility by projecting onto the constraint set at each denoising step in the image space.
The code for the Bastek and Kochmann [38] baseline has been implemented using their video denoising diffusion model approach for metamaterial inverse design.
The code for measuring FID score to evaluate image quality of generated microstructures has been implemented.
The code for measuring constraint violation rate (percentage of samples with porosity error > 10%) for the microstructure experiment has been implemented.
The code for measuring MSE between the generated structure's stress-strain response and the target stress-strain curve for the metamaterial experiment has been implemented.
The code for measuring the fraction of physically invalid shapes (shapes that violate physical feasibility constraints) in the metamaterial experiment has been implemented.
The code for measuring constraint satisfaction rate (percentage of samples that do not resemble copyrighted Mickey Mouse material) for the copyright experiment has been implemented.

The microstructure generation experiment for the Latent Constrained Model and all baselines (Cond, PDM) has been executed successfully without errors, generating samples at 1024x1024 resolution with specified porosity levels.
The metamaterial inverse design experiment for the Latent Constrained Model with DPO has been executed successfully without errors, running 5 DPO iterations per sample using the Abaqus FEM simulator.
The copyright-safe generation experiment for the Latent Constrained Model and all baselines (Cond, PDM) has been executed successfully without errors at 1024x1024 resolution.
The Bastek and Kochmann baseline has been executed successfully for the metamaterial experiment without errors.
The ResNet50 copyright classifier fine-tuning has been executed successfully, achieving accuracy greater than 87% on the held-out test set.
The execution outcomes match Figure 1: the proposed Latent Constrained Model achieves FID score of 13.5±3.1 and 0%±0 constraint violation rate (P error > 10%) for microstructure generation.

The execution outcomes match Figure 1: the Conditional Diffusion Model (Cond) baseline achieves FID score of 10.8±0.9 but 68.4%±12.4 constraint violation rate, demonstrating that text conditioning alone is insufficient for enforcing porosity constraints.

The execution outcomes match Figure 1: the Projected Diffusion Model (PDM) baseline achieves FID score of 30.7±6.8 with 0%±0 constraint violation rate, showing that PDM satisfies constraints but at significantly lower image quality.

The execution outcomes match Figure 2: the proposed Latent Constrained Model produces a void diameter distribution in generated microstructures that more closely resembles the ground truth training set distribution compared to the Conditional Diffusion Model baseline.

The execution outcomes confirm that the proposed method provides exact constraint satisfaction (0% violation) for the convex porosity constraint, consistent with the theoretical guarantees of Theorem 4.1 and Theorem G.1.

The execution outcomes match Figure 3: the proposed Latent Constrained Model achieves MSE of 1.4±0.6 for the stress-strain response matching task, representing a 4.6x improvement over Bastek and Kochmann (6.4±4.6) and a 5.1x improvement over Conditional Stable Diffusion (7.1±4.5).
The execution outcomes match Figure 3: the proposed method achieves only 5% physically invalid shapes, compared to 20% for Bastek and Kochmann and 55% for Conditional Stable Diffusion.

The execution outcomes match Figure 4: the DPO process progressively reduces the MSE from approximately 179.5 at step 0 to 175.6 at step 2 to 12.5 at step 4, demonstrating iterative stress-strain curve alignment.

The execution outcomes match Figure 7 (Appendix E.2): the proposed Latent Constrained Model outperforms baselines in both interpolation (MSE ~1.2) and extrapolation (MSE ~78.3) settings for the metamaterial experiment, while Cond (Stable Image) achieves ~7.0 and ~127.3 and Bastek and Kochmann achieves ~9.2 and ~99.6 respectively.

The execution outcomes match Figure 5 (bottom-right): the proposed Latent Constrained Model achieves 90% constraint satisfaction (copyright-safe generation rate) and FID score of 65.1 for the copyright experiment.

The execution outcomes match Figure 5 (bottom-right): the Conditional Diffusion Model baseline generates the copyrighted Mickey Mouse character 33% of the time despite text-conditioning, and PDM achieves only 71% constraint satisfaction with worse FID (75.3) due to limitations at high resolution.

The execution outcomes confirm that the PDM baseline failed to generate reasonable samples at 1024x1024 resolution for the copyright experiment, requiring downsampling to 64x64 with subsequent upscaling, resulting in higher FID scores.

The execution outcomes match Table 1 (Appendix F): constrained sampling runtime is approximately 50s for microstructure and 65s for copyright on Nvidia A100-SXM4-80GB, and each DPO iteration takes approximately 30s on CPU for metamaterials.
The execution outcomes confirm that the DPO convergence for metamaterials is achieved within 5 iterations totaling approximately 2.5 minutes per optimization run.
